reasoning: The Lean statement closely matches the informal text. `f : ℝ → ℝ` and `E : Set ℝ` encode "f real-valued on a set of reals" (with the graph as a subset of the plane). The hypothesis `IsCompact E` mirrors "E is compact". The set `G = {(x, f x) | x ∈ E}` is exactly the informal definition of the graph of f on E. The conclusion `ContinuousOn f E ↔ IsCompact G` matches "f is continuous on E iff its graph is compact". Although f is defined on all of ℝ in Lean rather than only on E, both `ContinuousOn f E` and the graph G only inspect f at points of E, so this encoding is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
